overview.title: Cybersecurity Tools & Resources
overview.description: Master penetration testing, network security, web application security, and ethical hacking with industry-standard tools and methodologies

resources.name: Burp Suite - Web Vulnerability Scanner
resources.shortDesc: Professional web vulnerability scanner and penetration testing tool for web applications
resources.fullDescription: Burp Suite is the leading platform for web application security testing. It combines automated scanning with manual testing tools to identify vulnerabilities like SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, and authentication flaws. Used by professional security testers and bug bounty hunters worldwide.

specifications.Free Tier: Full Community Edition with manual tools

bestPractices: Use Intruder for fuzzing and brute force

codeSnippet.title: Burp Suite API Example - Automated Scanning

resources.name: OWASP ZAP - Automated Security Scanner
resources.shortDesc: Free web application security scanner maintained by OWASP for finding vulnerabilities
resources.fullDescription: OWASP Zed Attack Proxy (ZAP) is a free, open-source web application security scanner. It's one of the most popular tools for finding vulnerabilities in web applications and APIs. ZAP combines automated scanning with a comprehensive set of manual tools for penetration testing.

codeSnippet.title: ZAP API Example - Automated Scanning
codeSnippet.code:
import requests

# ZAP API example
ZAP_API_URL = 'http://localhost:8080'
ZAP_API_KEY = 'your-api-key'

# Start spidering
target_url = 'https://example.com'
spider_scan = requests.get(
    f'{ZAP_API_URL}/JSON/spider/action/scan/',
    params={
        'apikey': ZAP_API_KEY,
        'url': target_url,
        'maxChildren': '10'
    }
)

scan_id = spider_scan.json()['scan']
print(f'Spider scan started: {scan_id}')

# Check spider status
status = requests.get(
    f'{ZAP_API_URL}/JSON/spider/view/status/',
    params={'apikey': ZAP_API_KEY, 'scanId': scan_id}
)
print(f'Spider progress: {status.json()["status"]}%')

# Start active scan
ascan = requests.get(
    f'{ZAP_API_URL}/JSON/ascan/action/scan/',
    params={
        'apikey': ZAP_API_KEY,
        'url': target_url,
        'recurse': 'true',
        'inScopeOnly': 'false'
    }
)
print(f'Active scan started: {ascan.json()["scan"]}')

resources.name: Metasploit Framework - Penetration Testing
resources.shortDesc: Comprehensive penetration testing framework with thousands of exploits and payloads
resources.fullDescription: Metasploit Framework is the world's most used penetration testing framework. It provides a complete environment for exploit development, payload generation, and vulnerability verification. Used by security professionals, researchers, and ethical hackers for comprehensive security assessments.

specifications.Platforms: Windows, Linux, macOS, Android, iOS, routers

codeSnippet.title: Metasploit Module Example
codeSnippet.code:
require 'msf/core'

class MetasploitModule < Msf::Exploit::Remote
  Rank = ExcellentRanking

  include Msf::Exploit::Remote::Tcp

  def initialize(info = {})
    super(update_info(info,
      'Name'           => 'Example Exploit',
      'Description'    => %q{
        Example Metasploit exploit module.
        Demonstrates basic structure.
      },
      'Author'         => [ 'Security Researcher' ],
      'References'     => [
        [ 'URL', 'https://example.com/vuln' ]
      ],
      'DisclosureDate' => '2023-01-01',
      'License'        => MSF_LICENSE,
      'Platform'       => 'unix',
      'Arch'           => ARCH_CMD,
      'Privileged'     => false,
      'Targets'        => [
        [ 'Automatic', {} ]
      ],
      'DefaultTarget'  => 0
    ))

    register_options([
      Opt::RPORT(80)
    ])
  end

  def exploit
    print_status("Connecting to #{rhost}:#{rport}")
    connect
    
    # Exploit code here
    
    handler
    disconnect
  end
end

resources.name: Wireshark - Network Protocol Analyzer
resources.shortDesc: World's foremost network protocol analyzer for network troubleshooting and security analysis
resources.fullDescription: Wireshark is the world's most widely-used network protocol analyzer. It lets you capture and interactively browse the traffic running on a computer network. Used for network troubleshooting, analysis, software and protocol development, and education.
bestPractices: Use capture filters to reduce data
Use color rules for quick identification

codeSnippet.title: Wireshark Command Line Examples
codeSnippet.code:
# Capture traffic on interface eth0
sudo tshark -i eth0 -w capture.pcap

# Capture only HTTP traffic
sudo tshark -i eth0 -f 'tcp port 80' -w http_capture.pcap

# Read and filter existing capture
sudo tshark -r capture.pcap -Y 'http.request.method == GET'

# Extract HTTP URLs from capture
tshark -r capture.pcap -T fields -e http.request.full_uri | head -10

# DNS query analysis
tshark -r capture.pcap -Y 'dns.flags.response == 0' \
  -T fields -e dns.qry.name -e dns.qry.type

# TCP stream reassembly
tshark -r capture.pcap -q -z follow,tcp,ascii,0

# Protocol statistics
tshark -r capture.pcap -q -z io,phs

resources.name: Nmap - Network Discovery & Security Scanning
resources.shortDesc: Powerful network discovery and security auditing tool for mapping networks and identifying vulnerabilities
resources.fullDescription: Nmap (Network Mapper) is a free and open-source utility for network discovery and security auditing. It uses raw IP packets to determine what hosts are available on the network, what services they are offering, what operating systems they are running, and what type of packet filters/firewalls are in use.

specifications.Scan Types: TCP SYN, TCP connect, UDP, ICMP, etc.
specifications.Host Discovery: ARP, ICMP, TCP SYN, TCP ACK, UDP, SCTP

bestPractices: Use -T4 for faster scans on good networks

codeSnippet.title: Nmap Scanning Examples
codeSnippet.code:
# Basic host discovery
nmap -sn 192.168.1.0/24

# TCP SYN scan (stealth)
nmap -sS 192.168.1.100

# Service and version detection
nmap -sV 192.168.1.100

# OS fingerprinting
nmap -O 192.168.1.100

# Comprehensive scan
nmap -sS -sV -O -p- 192.168.1.100

# Aggressive scan with scripts
nmap -A 192.168.1.100

# Scan multiple targets
nmap 192.168.1.100,101,102

# Scan from file
nmap -iL targets.txt

# UDP scan
nmap -sU 192.168.1.100

# Firewall evasion
nmap -sS -D RND:10 192.168.1.100

# Vulnerability scanning
nmap --script vuln 192.168.1.100

# Web server enumeration
nmap --script http-enum 192.168.1.100

resources.name: Kali Linux - Penetration Testing Distribution
resources.shortDesc: Debian-based Linux distribution for penetration testing, ethical hacking, and digital forensics
resources.fullDescription: Kali Linux is a Debian-based Linux distribution designed for digital forensics and penetration testing. It comes pre-installed with hundreds of security tools for various information security tasks such as penetration testing, security research, computer forensics, and reverse engineering.
bestPractices: Use in virtual machines for safety
Learn one tool at a time

codeSnippet.title: Kali Linux Essential Commands
codeSnippet.code:
# Update Kali Linux
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade

# Install additional tools
sudo apt install nmap wireshark metasploit-framework

# Start Metasploit
sudo msfdb init
sudo msfconsole

# Basic Nmap scan
sudo nmap -sS -A target-ip

# Start Wireshark (GUI)
sudo wireshark

# Or use tshark (command line)
sudo tshark -i eth0

# Password cracking with Hashcat
hashcat -m 0 -a 3 hashes.txt ?a?a?a?a?a?a

# Web application scanning
nikto -h http://target-website.com

# SQL injection testing
sqlmap -u 'http://vulnerable-site.com/page?id=1'

# Wireless network scanning
sudo airodump-ng wlan0

# Forensic image acquisition
dcfldd if=/dev/sda of=forensic-image.dd hash=md5,sha256

resources.name: Hashcat - Advanced Password Recovery
resources.shortDesc: World's fastest password cracker supporting multiple hashing algorithms and attack modes
resources.fullDescription: Hashcat is the world's fastest and most advanced password recovery utility. It supports five unique attack modes for cracking passwords and can work with hundreds of hashing algorithms. Used by security professionals, penetration testers, and forensic investigators.

specifications.Performance: Up to 100s of billions of hashes/second
specifications.Distributed: Hashcat can be distributed across multiple GPUs

codeSnippet.title: Hashcat Usage Examples
codeSnippet.code:
# Basic dictionary attack
hashcat -m 0 -a 0 hashes.txt wordlist.txt

# Brute force attack (8 characters)
hashcat -m 0 -a 3 hashes.txt ?a?a?a?a?a?a?a?a

# Mask attack (password format: Password + 2 digits)
hashcat -m 0 -a 3 hashes.txt Password?d?d

# Hybrid attack (wordlist + 2 digits)
hashcat -m 0 -a 6 hashes.txt wordlist.txt ?d?d

# Rule-based attack
hashcat -m 0 -a 0 hashes.txt wordlist.txt -r rules/best64.rule

# GPU acceleration (device 1)
hashcat -m 0 -a 0 -d 1 hashes.txt wordlist.txt

# Show cracked passwords
hashcat -m 0 --show hashes.txt

# Benchmark performance
hashcat -b

# WPA2 cracking
hashcat -m 2500 capture.hccapx wordlist.txt

# NTLM cracking
hashcat -m 1000 ntlm_hashes.txt wordlist.txt

# MD5 cracking
hashcat -m 0 md5_hashes.txt rockyou.txt

resources.name: John the Ripper - Password Cracker
resources.shortDesc: Fast password cracker supporting hundreds of hash and cipher types for password recovery
resources.fullDescription: John the Ripper is a fast password cracker, currently available for many flavors of Unix, Windows, DOS, and OpenVMS. Its primary purpose is to detect weak Unix passwords, but it supports hundreds of hash and cipher types including DES, MD5, Blowfish, Kerberos, and Windows NT/2000/XP/2003 LM hashes.

codeSnippet.title: John the Ripper Usage Examples
codeSnippet.code:
# Basic password cracking
john hashes.txt

# Use specific wordlist
john --wordlist=rockyou.txt hashes.txt

# Show cracked passwords
john --show hashes.txt

# Single crack mode (uses GECOS info)
john --single hashes.txt

# Incremental mode (brute force)
john --incremental hashes.txt

# External mode with custom rules
john --external=CustomMode hashes.txt

# Multi-core processing
john --fork=4 hashes.txt

# Format specification
john --format=md5 hashes.txt

# Session management
john --session=my_session hashes.txt
john --restore=my_session

# Rules for word mangling
john --rules --wordlist=wordlist.txt hashes.txt

# Status checking
john --status

# Benchmarking
john --test
